findings.title: Flow mismatch: EX-COB-001 `affects` vs process-step transitions
findings.detail: its `affects` lists PS-COB-001, PS-COB-002, but no process-step transitions into it.

findings.title: Flow mismatch: EX-COB-002 `affects` vs process-step transitions
findings.detail: its `affects` lists PS-COB-002, but no process-step transitions into it.

findings.title: Flow mismatch: EX-COB-003 `affects` vs process-step transitions
findings.detail: its `affects` lists PS-COB-003, but no process-step transitions into it.

findings.title: Flow mismatch: EX-COB-004 `affects` vs process-step transitions
findings.detail: its `affects` lists PS-COB-004, but no process-step transitions into it.

findings.title: Flow mismatch: EX-COB-005 `affects` vs process-step transitions
findings.detail: its `affects` lists PS-COB-002, but no process-step transitions into it.

findings.title: SYS-COB-001 claims steps that do not list it
findings.detail: SYS-COB-001 (CRM) lists all six process steps and its prose says every step reads and updates the CRM case, but PS-COB-002, PS-COB-003, PS-COB-004 and PS-COB-006 do not include SYS-COB-001 in their systems field.

findings.title: SYS-COB-002 claims steps that do not list it
findings.detail: SYS-COB-002 (OWS) lists all six process steps and its prose says it drives the sequence and timing for all six, but PS-COB-002, PS-COB-003, PS-COB-004 and PS-COB-005 do not include SYS-COB-002 in their systems field.

findings.title: SYS-COB-007 claims PS-COB-002, which does not list it
findings.detail: SYS-COB-007 (DMS) lists PS-COB-002 among its steps, but PS-COB-002's systems field names only SYS-COB-003 and SYS-COB-004.

findings.title: Credit Assessment step has no control linked
findings.detail: No control element links to PS-COB-003. Every other step that carries operational risk has at least one control (CP-COB-003 on PS-COB-001, CP-COB-001/002 on PS-COB-002, CP-COB-004 on PS-COB-004, CP-COB-005 on PS-COB-005), yet the credit decision — where the Credit Analyst approves a limit within their own authority — is governed by no documented control.

findings.title: Segregation-of-duties policy relies on a four-eyes check that is not a documented control
findings.detail: REG-COB-006 (SoD Policy) has an empty controls list and states it is met through the four-eyes check performed during Account Setup (PS-COB-004), but no control element captures that four-eyes check. CG-COB-002 notes the same check as a missing control, framed as account-configuration verification.

findings.title: Friction point FP-COB-004 is linked to an unrelated pain point
findings.detail: FP-COB-004 (Complex Ownership Questions, root cause: KYC questions written in regulatory rather than client language) sets painPoint PP-COB-003, but PP-COB-003 is 'KYC screening false positives' — a screening-threshold problem. The two describe different issues, and no pain point captures the ownership-question complexity.

findings.title: FP-COB-004 has no addressedBy back-link
findings.detail: II-COB-007 addresses FP-COB-004, but FP-COB-004 carries no addressedBy field — unlike FP-COB-001, FP-COB-002 and FP-COB-003, which each link back to the idea addressing them.

findings.title: PS-COB-001 contains stray text
findings.detail: The 'What happens' block of PS-COB-001 ends with the stray characters 'fghfh' appended to its final sentence — an apparent accidental edit.

findings.title: EX-COB-005 names an undocumented role
findings.detail: EX-COB-005 is handled by a 'Senior KYC Analyst', but no role element documents that role, even though it performs enhanced due diligence on roughly 20% of applications.

findings.title: Pain point PP-COB-002 understates the number of systems
findings.detail: PP-COB-002 says staff move between 'six or more systems' and enumerates six (CRM, OWS, KYC platform, screening, core banking, card management), but eight systems are documented for the process — the enumeration omits SYS-COB-006 (CDS) and SYS-COB-007 (DMS).

findings.title: Touchpoint 'Receive account activation' is mapped to the wrong step
findings.detail: JT-COB-004 (Receive account activation) sets occursAt PS-COB-004 (Account Setup & Configuration), but it describes the bank sending the activation notice with access details — work that PS-COB-005 (Client Communication & Activation) explicitly owns. PS-COB-004's prose describes no client-facing communication.

findings.title: Cycle-time metric conflicts with the per-step SLAs
findings.detail: M-COB-002 gives the processing time from application receipt to a live account as 4-6 hours, but the step SLAs spanning that window are denominated in business days — PS-COB-002 at 2-5 business days and PS-COB-003 at 3 business days alone far exceed a 4-6 hour total.

findings.title: Complexity figures disagree between the metric and the exception
findings.detail: M-COB-003 puts the complex-application share at 30%, defining complex as multi-layer ownership, trusts or foreign shareholders. EX-COB-005 uses the same definition but states the complex-ownership exception occurs on about 20% of applications.

findings.title: Web-sourced innovation ideas name a trend in prose but carry no fromTrend link
findings.detail: II-COB-004, II-COB-005, II-COB-006 and II-COB-007 each state in their source line that they were derived from a market trend (TR-COB-004, TR-COB-001, TR-COB-003, and TR-COB-001/TR-COB-004 respectively), but their fromTrend relation is empty — unlike the newer II-COB-008 to II-COB-011, which populate it. The trend-to-idea traceability is missing from the structured relation.

findings.title: Who owns control CP-COB-004?
findings.detail: CP-COB-004 names its owner as 'IT/Operations', which is not a documented role, while ROLE-COB-001 (Operations Officer) lists CP-COB-004 among its controls. Which role owns the control?

findings.title: Is the external credit bureau a missing system and integration?
findings.detail: PS-COB-003 relies on external bureau data and PP-COB-005 describes a fire-and-forget bureau integration, but no system element documents the external credit bureau and SYS-COB-006 (CDS) has an empty integrates list — unlike the comparable external screening dependency documented as SYS-COB-004 and integration INT-COB-002. Should a system and integration be added?

findings.title: Should PG-COB-001 and PG-COB-003 be consolidated?
findings.detail: PG-COB-001 (SLA adherence not measured) and PG-COB-003 (per-step processing time not captured) both call for capturing per-step start/end times in OWS, and innovation idea II-COB-009 addresses the two together as a single dashboard. Are these one gap or two?
